description: Canonical restaurant operations ontology. Each metric has a stable id, human label, the synonyms/aliases used across POS exports, the formula (if derived), the valid sane range used for sanity-checking uploads, and the unit/format. This is the durable, accreting asset: every new POS quirk and customer correction lands here, not in code.

location.label: Location
location.description: A single physical restaurant unit within the operator's group.
location.notes: Multi-location operators are the core user. Location is the primary slicing dimension for almost every comparison.

date.label: Business Date
date.description: The operating/business date a record belongs to. NOT the same as the transaction timestamp — a sale at 1am may belong to the prior business date depending on the day-part close.
date.notes: Toast exports 'Business Date'; Square uses 'Date'; Clover uses 'Date' or a full timestamp that must be truncated to a calendar day.

daypart.label: Daypart
daypart.description: Service period bucket — breakfast, lunch, dinner, late night.

menu_item.label: Menu Item
menu_item.description: An individually sold item on the menu. The unit of product-mix (PMIX) analysis.

menu_category.label: Menu Category
menu_category.description: Grouping of menu items — Apps, Entrees, Beverages, Alcohol, Desserts.

revenue_center.label: Revenue Center
revenue_center.description: Physical or operational zone within the restaurant (e.g., Dining Room, Bar, Patio, Drive-Thru).
revenue_center.notes: Toast handles this natively (Revenue Center). Square and Clover frequently require mapping via device names or custom service charges.

order_source.label: Order Source / Channel
order_source.description: The inbound channel of the transaction — Dine-In, Takeout, Native Online, or Third-Party Delivery.
order_source.notes: Critical for cross-referencing high-margin native sales against commission-heavy marketplace apps. Toast='Dining Option', Square='Fulfillment', Clover='Order Type'.

employee.label: Employee / Server
employee.description: The staff member tied to a specific sales transaction or clocked labor shift.

order_status.label: Order Status
order_status.description: Lifecycle state of an order (Open, Paid, Refunded, Partially Paid, Voided). MUST be used to filter to settled revenue before summing — counting Open/Refunded rows overstates sales.
order_status.notes: Clover is ledger-grain and includes non-Paid rows. Filter to Paid (and net out Refunded) before any revenue rollup. Square encodes this via Refunded/Partial Refund lines; Toast exports are typically already settled.

payment_type.label: Payment / Tender Type
payment_type.description: Settlement medium — Cash, Visa, Amex, Gift Card, External. Used for tender mix and processing-fee analysis.

modifier.label: Modifier
modifier.description: A change/addition appended to a base item (e.g., Meat Temp, Add Bacon, Size). The grouping is the modifier group.

net_sales.label: Net Sales
net_sales.description: Revenue from food and beverage sales AFTER discounts/comps and BEFORE tax. The denominator for nearly every cost percentage. Almost all restaurant KPIs are expressed as a % of net sales, not gross.
net_sales.notes: If both gross and net are present, OpticAI uses net for all percentage denominators and says so. Confusing gross for net silently overstates margins — a classic amateur mistake.

gross_sales.label: Gross Sales
gross_sales.description: Revenue BEFORE discounts and comps. Higher than net sales. Should not be used as the denominator for cost percentages.

direct_labor.label: Direct (Operational) Labor Cost
direct_labor.description: The unburdened raw cost of scheduled shifts: hourly wages, shift pay, and salaried floor-management pay tied to running the floor. This is the daily scheduling-efficiency number. Distinct from fully-burdened labor (see labor_burden). See calc_rules labor_basis.
direct_labor.notes: If the operator's labor_basis is 'fully_burdened' but only this direct figure is present, gross up via labor_burden_multiplier and label the result an estimate.

labor_burden.label: Labor Burden
labor_burden.description: Overhead costs of putting employees on the schedule, on top of direct wages: payroll taxes (FICA, Medicare, FUTA), benefits (health, 401k match), workers' comp, PTO, and uniforms. Adds roughly 15-25% to base wages.

foh_labor.label: Front of House Labor
foh_labor.description: Labor cost for servers, hosts, and bartenders. Lower base operational wage % of sales because FOH relies heavily on tips.

boh_labor.label: Back of House (Heart of House) Labor
boh_labor.description: Labor cost for chefs, line cooks, and dishwashers. Typically a HIGHER labor % of sales than FOH.

labor_pct.label: Labor %
labor_pct.description: Labor cost as a percentage of net sales. The single most-watched controllable cost line. Resolve to the operator's declared basis (direct vs. fully-burdened) and always state which.
labor_pct.notes: Benchmarks here are direct/operational labor. Fully-burdened runs ~4-6 points higher. If a direct labor_pct column exists, prefer it only if it agrees with computed value within ~1.5 points; otherwise flag — usually a different sales_basis or labor definition.

food_cost.label: Food Cost (COGS)
food_cost.description: STRICTLY the edible ingredients (proteins, produce, dairy, dry goods, spices). NOT the same as total COGS — Food Cost excludes beverage and paper/packaging. See calc_rules food_cost_definition. QSR/fast-casual may include packaging (set food_cost_definition=includes_paper).
food_cost.notes: If a column is labeled 'COGS' it may be TOTAL COGS (food+beverage+paper), not food cost alone. Confirm before treating it as food cost.

total_cogs.label: Total COGS
total_cogs.description: Sum of Food Cost + Beverage Cost + Paper/Packaging Cost. The full cost of goods sold. Distinct from Food Cost alone.

beverage_cost.label: Beverage Cost
beverage_cost.description: Cost of beverages, tracked SEPARATELY from food. Commonly segmented into soda/water (N/A), beer, wine, and liquor. Alcohol is ALWAYS beverage, never food cost.
beverage_cost.notes: Pour costs differ by type: liquor ~18-20%, beer ~24-28%, wine ~30-40%, soda <10%. Segment where possible.

beverage_sales.label: Beverage Sales
beverage_sales.description: Revenue from beverages — the correct denominator for beverage/pour cost %, not total sales.

paper_packaging.label: Paper & Packaging Cost
paper_packaging.description: Direct disposables that leave the building with food: to-go boxes, delivery bags, napkins, straws, utensils. Belongs in COGS as a separate line, NOT in Food Cost. Indirect supplies (restroom towels, kitchen trash bags, guest checks) are Operating Expenses, not COGS.
paper_packaging.notes: Target ~3-4% of net sales for limited-service, ~1-2% for full-service.

food_cost_pct.label: Food Cost %
food_cost_pct.description: Food cost (edible only) as a percentage of net sales. Distinct from total COGS %.

beverage_cost_pct.label: Beverage (Pour) Cost %
beverage_cost_pct.description: Beverage cost as a percentage of BEVERAGE sales (not total sales).

total_cogs_pct.label: Total COGS %
total_cogs_pct.description: Food + beverage + paper/packaging as a percentage of net sales.

prime_cost_pct.label: Prime Cost %
prime_cost_pct.description: Labor % + COGS % combined. The headline controllable-cost number. Some operators use labor + food cost only; others use labor + total COGS. Confirm which. Rule of thumb: keep under 60-65% of net sales for full service.
prime_cost_pct.formula: labor_pct + total_cogs_pct (or food_cost_pct per operator definition)
prime_cost_pct.notes: Prime cost above ~70% of net sales is a serious profitability warning in full-service. This is the number most operators check first.

check_count.label: Check Count
check_count.description: Number of guest checks/tickets closed. NOT the same as guest count (covers) — one check can cover multiple guests.
check_count.notes: Square's 'Transactions' usually means checks, not covers. This is a frequent ambiguity — confirm with user when the column is just 'transactions' or 'count'.

guest_count.label: Guest Count (Covers)
guest_count.description: Number of individual guests served (covers). Used for per-person spend and capacity analysis.
guest_count.notes: Square and Clover often do NOT track covers natively. If absent, per-guest metrics fall back to per-check.

average_check.label: Average Check
average_check.description: Net sales divided by number of checks. The per-ticket spend.
average_check.notes: PPA (per-person average) uses guest_count instead of check_count. If only checks are available, label the metric 'average check' not 'per-person average' to stay honest.

comps.label: Comps & Discounts
comps.description: Dollar value of complimentary items and discounts. High comps can mask service problems or theft.
comps.notes: Comps/discounts are items that WERE made and given away or price-reduced. Distinct from voids (rung but never made). High comps can mask service problems or theft.

comps_pct.label: Comps %
comps_pct.description: Comps and discounts as a percentage of gross or net sales.
comps_pct.notes: Comps above ~5% of sales warrant investigation — could be service recovery, manager abuse, or theft.

overtime_hours.label: Overtime Hours
overtime_hours.description: Hours paid at the overtime premium. A primary driver of labor cost overruns.

labor_hours.label: Labor Hours
labor_hours.description: Total hours worked across all staff for the period.

sales_per_labor_hour.label: Sales per Labor Hour (SPLH)
sales_per_labor_hour.description: Net sales divided by labor hours. A productivity measure independent of wage rates.

quantity_sold.label: Quantity Sold
quantity_sold.description: Units of a menu item sold. The basis of PMIX analysis.

item_revenue.label: Item Revenue
item_revenue.description: Revenue attributable to a single menu item — used in PMIX to rank menu performance.

food_cost_per_item.label: Item Food Cost
food_cost_per_item.description: The plate cost / recipe cost of a single menu item. Combined with price to compute item margin.

item_margin.label: Item Contribution Margin
item_margin.description: Price minus plate cost — the gross profit contributed by each unit sold. Drives menu engineering (stars/plowhorses/puzzles/dogs).

waste.label: Waste / Spoilage
waste.description: Quantity or dollar value of product wasted, spoiled, or thrown out.

inventory_variance.label: Inventory Variance
inventory_variance.description: Difference between theoretical (recipe-based) usage and actual usage. High variance signals waste, over-portioning, or theft.
inventory_variance.notes: Variance beyond +/- a few percent on a high-cost item (proteins especially) is a top diagnostic flag.

guest_satisfaction.label: Guest Satisfaction Score
guest_satisfaction.description: Aggregate guest rating — could be a star rating, NPS, or survey score depending on source.
guest_satisfaction.notes: Scale varies (1-5 stars, 0-10 NPS, 0-100). Detect scale from value range before interpreting.

tips.label: Total Tips
tips.description: Gratuities paid by guests to front-of-house staff. Must be rigorously EXCLUDED from Net Sales to prevent margin inflation.
tips.notes: Square and Clover often bundle tips into a 'Total Collected' / 'Total Paid' figure — isolate tips first. Tips are NOT operational revenue.

service_charges.label: Service Charges
service_charges.description: Mandatory fees added to checks (large-party auto-gratuity, delivery fees, wellness fees). Unlike tips, these count as operational revenue before distribution.
service_charges.notes: Distinct from tips. Auto-gratuity on large parties usually flows here, not into Tips.

tax.label: Sales Tax Collected
tax.description: Total state and local sales tax collected on transactions. Pass-through liability — must be isolated from core operational revenue.
tax.notes: Never include tax in net sales. It is owed to the taxing authority, not the operator.

voids.label: Voids
voids.description: Financial value of items rung into the system but removed BEFORE production/fulfillment. No food was made; no revenue was generated. Distinct from comps.
voids.notes: Differentiate from Comps (made & given away). High voids are a classic proxy for server-training gaps, system friction, or potential fraud.

labor_cost_per_hour.label: Average Hourly Labor Rate
labor_cost_per_hour.description: Blended cost of labor per hour worked across all positions during the period.

turnover_rate.label: Staff Turnover Rate
turnover_rate.description: Percentage of employees who leave within a given timeframe. An HR/scheduling metric, not a native POS export.
turnover_rate.notes: Restaurant turnover runs high (often 70-100%+ annually). Rarely present in POS exports — usually from payroll/HR or a 3rd-party labor integration.

paper_cost_per_item.label: Item Paper/Packaging Cost
paper_cost_per_item.description: Cost of packaging, bags, and disposables associated with a specific menu item. Used for off-premise PMIX modeling.
paper_cost_per_item.notes: Item-grain cousin of paper_packaging (which is period-grain). Apply per recipe-card rule: include only if the item ALWAYS requires it.

beverage_cost_per_item.label: Item Beverage Cost
beverage_cost_per_item.description: Exact cost of ingredients to produce a single beverage unit (pour cost per drink).

theoretical_cogs.label: Theoretical COGS
theoretical_cogs.description: What COGS SHOULD be based purely on recipe item costs * item sales quantities, assuming zero waste or theft. Gap vs. actual COGS = the variance to investigate.

refunds.label: Refunds
refunds.description: Money returned to guests AFTER a sale closed (full or partial reversals). Reduces net sales for the period. Distinct from voids (pre-production) and comps (made & given away).
refunds.notes: Square reports refunds as separate post-hoc lines that must be subtracted. Clover encodes refund state in Order Status (=Refunded/Partially Paid). Toast usually nets them already — confirm to avoid double-counting.

modifier_revenue.label: Modifier Revenue
modifier_revenue.description: Incremental revenue from item modifiers/add-ons (extra protein, upsized, premium options). Can be a meaningful slice of sales and is handled differently by each POS.
modifier_revenue.notes: Square/Clover attach modifier dollars at line-item grain; Toast tracks modifier names/groups but revenue often rolls into the parent item. Don't drop these — they are real sales.

cash_tips.label: Declared Cash Tips
cash_tips.description: Tips self-reported as cash at clock-out. Subset of total tips; relevant for tip-credit and wage-compliance, not operational revenue.
cash_tips.notes: Part of tip composition. Never include in net sales.

card_tips.label: Credit Card Tips
card_tips.description: Tips captured digitally via card. Subset of total tips. Combined with cash tips = total tip pool.
card_tips.notes: Clover tracks 'Tips Owed' (processed) vs 'Tips Paid' (cashed out) on the labor side.

order_duration.label: Order Duration (Minutes)
order_duration.description: Minutes from check open to payment. A throughput/table-turn proxy.

on_hand.label: On Hand
on_hand.description: Exact quantity of an inventory item currently in your building, physically available to be prepared or sold to customers. Snapshot count taken during a physical inventory count.
on_hand.notes: Compare against par_level to identify items running low. Variance = on_hand minus theoretical_qty.

par_level.label: Par Level
par_level.description: Target stock quantity for an inventory item — the minimum amount you want on hand before triggering a reorder. Set by operators based on usage rate and lead time.
par_level.notes: Items where on_hand < par_level need to be ordered. Items consistently far above par_level may be over-ordered.

inventory_unit_cost.label: Unit Cost
inventory_unit_cost.description: Cost per unit of an inventory item (e.g. cost per pound, per case, per each). Used to calculate total inventory value and theoretical food cost.
inventory_unit_cost.notes: Multiply by on_hand or usage to calculate dollar value of stock or variance.

inventory_variance_pct.label: Inventory Variance %
inventory_variance_pct.description: Percentage difference between theoretical usage (based on recipe and sales) and actual usage (based on physical counts). High variance indicates waste, theft, or portioning issues.
inventory_variance_pct.notes: Positive variance = used more than expected (waste/theft/over-portioning). Negative variance = used less than expected (under-portioning or count error).

actual_usage.label: Actual Usage
actual_usage.description: Actual quantity of an item consumed during a period, calculated from physical inventory counts: beginning_on_hand + purchases - ending_on_hand.
actual_usage.notes: Compare to theoretical_usage to compute variance.

theoretical_usage.label: Theoretical Usage
theoretical_usage.description: Expected quantity of an item that should have been consumed based on menu sales volume and standard recipe yield, assuming perfect portioning with no waste.
theoretical_usage.notes: Derived from PMIX × recipe yield. Variance from actual_usage reveals operational inefficiencies.

inventory_item.label: Inventory Item
inventory_item.description: Name of an ingredient or supply item tracked in inventory (e.g. Chicken Breast, House Blend Coffee, Paper Cups).
inventory_item.notes: Identifier — do not aggregate or sum. Use as a dimension for filtering and grouping.

inventory_unit.label: Unit of Measure
inventory_unit.description: The unit in which an inventory item is counted or purchased (e.g. lbs, oz, each, case, gallon).
inventory_unit.notes: Identifier — context for on_hand and usage numbers. Never compare on_hand values across different units without conversion.

order_id.label: Order ID
order_id.description: Unique id for an order/transaction block. PRIMARY JOIN KEY for unifying Clover's separate Order/Line-Item/Employee logs and Toast Order/Check tables.

check_id.label: Check ID
check_id.description: Unique id for an individual check within an order (multiple checks can split one order).

item_id.label: Item ID / SKU / PLU
item_id.description: Catalog identifier for a menu item — internal id, SKU, or PLU. Join key between PMIX and inventory/costing.

employee_id.label: Employee ID
employee_id.description: Unique payroll/system id for a team member. Join key between labor shifts and sales attribution.

business_timestamp.label: Transaction Timestamp
business_timestamp.description: Full datetime an order opened/closed or a shift started/ended. Source for deriving Business Date on Square/Clover (truncate + apply close-hour rule). NOT the business date itself.
business_timestamp.notes: On Square/Clover, business date must be derived from these via business_day_close_hour. Do not use raw timestamp as the period key.
